title: Chassalia subnuda
taxonomy: Chassalia subnuda is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Chassalia, which is a group of plants that are native to tropical regions. The plant's classification places it within the order Gentianales, a large group of flowering plants that also includes coffee, gardenias, and honeysuckles. Chassalia subnuda is further classified as a member of the family Rubiaceae, which includes a vast array of plants commonly known as coffee or madder family. The plant's classification is an essential aspect of understanding its evolutionary history and relationships with other organisms.

intro: Chassalia subnuda, also known as the hairy Chassalia, is a plant species in the Rubiaceae family, native to tropical regions of Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in treating various ailments such as fever, skin infections, and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Chassalia subnuda include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Chassalia subnuda is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into salves and ointments for topical application.
